indexes.name: idx_lineitem_shipdate_orderkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Supports TPC-H style date-range scans on lineitem and subsequent joins/aggregations involving l_orderkey (Q1, Q3, Q4, Q5, Q6, Q13, Q14).
indexes.benefits: Improves pruning of lineitem by ship date and speeds access to lineitem rows used for joining/grouping by order.

indexes.name: idx_lineitem_orderkey_commit_receipt
indexes.workload_pattern: Targets order-level correlated EXISTS/NOT EXISTS logic comparing commit/receipt dates within the same l_orderkey (Q11, Q20, Q22).
indexes.benefits: Enables more efficient lookups and range filtering for predicates on l_commitdate/l_receiptdate scoped by l_orderkey.
indexes.tradeoffs: Larger composite index with additional write overhead.

indexes.name: idx_orders_orderdate_orderpriority
indexes.workload_pattern: Supports queries that filter orders by o_orderdate range and then group/aggregate by o_orderpriority (Q22).
indexes.benefits: Speeds range-restricted aggregation when the grouping key is included in the access path.

indexes.name: idx_customer_mktsegment_custkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Optimizes selective filtering on customer segment plus join to orders via c_custkey (Q3).
indexes.benefits: Reduces the number of customer rows participating in joins for the targeted segment.

global_observations: The workload is dominated by TPC-H predicates on lineitem.l_shipdate and orders.o_orderdate, plus correlated EXISTS/NOT EXISTS predicates using lineitem.l_orderkey with l_commitdate/l_receiptdate comparisons.
Existing indexes include lineitem(l_shipdate) and orders(o_orderdate); proposed composites aim to extend these patterns with join keys and correlated predicate columns.
